source_pr.title: test: fix unit test TestInDecimal
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
fix unit test `TestInDecimal`


### What is changed and how it works?
when a float64 values is small enough, the `fmt` will format is like 「3.242530532696997e-05」 thus the 「0」 appended will contribute to the exponents instead of fraction cause parse decimal return error 「`ErrTruncated`」

close #14758  


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 - Integration test
 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
 - No code

symptoms: PR body: small float64 may format like "3.2425e-05"; appending "00" makes exponent parse fail (ErrTruncated).
failure_signature: PR body: small float64 may format like "3.2425e-05"; appending "00" makes exponent parse fail (ErrTruncated).
root_cause_explanation: Insufficient evidence to determine the underlying root cause from the available case data; needs CI logs or a reliable repro.

evidence: evidence: PR body: small float64 may format like "3.2425e-05"; appending "00" makes exponent parse fail (ErrTruncated).

evidence: diff changes random value generation from fmt.Sprintf("%v", float64(rand.Intn(1000))+rand.Float64()) to fmt.Sprintf("%d.%d", rand.Intn(1000), rand.Int31()) to avoid scientific notation (expression/builtin_other_vec_test.go).
